user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Blue Yonder Airlines.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 16:39 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines pipeline hygiene

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 09:27 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-01-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines)

[00:02:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:08:00] Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:14:00] Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:21:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:31:00] Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:36:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:39:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.
